text:
Admission Date:  2019-11-23              Discharge Date:   1976-02-28

Date of Birth:  2009-06-02             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Susan
Chief Complaint:
unresponsiveness

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Intubation

History of Present Illness:
35y/o M w/ h/o Major Depression, Psychosis from amphetamine use,
recurrent skin cellulitis, PSA (cocaine, methamphetamine, GHB),
MRSA colonization, was in USOH and went to acupuncture today
where he became unresponsive. People there called 911 and was
trasported here to Perry Memorial Hospital. In Key, Emma, report of ED resident
patient was not responding to any stimuli and did not have a gag
reflex. For concern of airway compromise, patient was intubated,
given narcan 2.4mg without response, D50, activated charcoal
then brought up to MICU. Additionally, the medical staff in the
ED mentioned that he might have overdosed on GHB.

We subsequently found out that patient had been taking GHB 15ml
6-7 times a day and that today he took 25ml prior to going to
acupunture.

Past Medical History:
1. Folliculitis
2. Occipital Cellulitis
3. R groin Cellulitis
4. MRSA colonized
5. Major Depression
6. Amphetamine psychosis
7. Poly substance abuse

Social History:
12yrs of tobacco 1/2ppd, +etoh, +PSA-amphetamines, cocaine, GHB.
No IVDU, MSM, lives in 423 Williams Roads
Robersonview, AL 27002-0813.

Family History:
Father died at age 43 from brain aneuysm. Mother is 68 y/o with
HTN and thyroid disease.

Physical Exam:
99.6 P: 104, BP: 155/63, R: 18 Vent: A/C 550x18, 50% and PEEP 5

GEN: on propofol, unresponsive to commands but moving all
extremities and jerking head.
HEENT: NC/AT, Pupils round and symmetric/reactive to light 3mm,
ETT in place
NECK: no JVP
CV: Tachy, RR, no m/r/g
PULM: CTA b/l, no w/r/r, mechanical breath sounds.
ABD: +BS, soft, flat, NT/ND
Ext: no c/c/e, DP/PT  2+ b/l
NEURO: moves all ext, respond to pain, pupils reactive to light,
patient sedated further exam deferred .
Skin: right lower ext skin abrasions, face with right and left
cheek eschars.
Access: left EJ, 2 PIV in right arm and 1 PIV in L arm


Pertinent Results:
WBC 8.4, HCT 38.7, Plt 215, Na 144, K 4.5, Cl 109, Bicarb 22,
BUN 23, Cr 1.0, Glu 105, AG 13,  UA: glu 250, ket 15,

VIT B12-424 FOLATE-12.4, TSH-3.1, DIGOXIN-<0.2* THEOPHYL-<0.8*,
PHENOBARB-<1.2* PHENYTOIN-<0.6* LITHIUM-LESS THAN
VALPROATE-<3.0*, ASA-NEG ETHANOL-NEG CARBAMZPN-<1.0*
ACETMNPHN-NEG bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG tricyclic-NEG

Utox: pos for cocaine and amphetamines.

CXR: no acute cp process
CT head: no ICH, masses

Brief Hospital Course:
35y/o M with h/o Major Depression, Amphetamine associated
psychosis, recurrent cellulitis,  PSA (GHB, Cocaine, [**Female First Name (un) **]
amphetamines), MRSA colonization p/w unresponsiveness 04-08 GHB
overdose.

1. Unresponsiveness: 04-08 GHB overdose
Patient was successfully extubated same day of admission as
there were no pulmonary issues and patient was alert and
responsive.
04-08 drug overdose from GHB-patient took 10 ml more of the amount
that he usually requires to get rid of his MRSA infection that
he states have "overwhelmed his body."
Patient was placed on Ativan per CIWA scale which he did not
require and was given Haldol 2mg TID and 5mg Kirby Medical Center prn per
psychiatry.  Patient spend the night at the medical floor,
continued to do well, CIWA scale discontinued.  There were no
active medical issues at the time of disharge.

2. GHB overdose:
Placed on ativan per CIWA which he did not use. No evidence of
withdrawal. Stable. psych team aware.

3. Psychosis:
ContactJeffrey Taylor psychiatry given his false belief system that MRSA
caused him to become unresponsive and that MRSA is killing him.
They came to assess patient and recommended inpatient treatment.
They suggested starting patient on Haldol 2mg tid and 5mg Kirby Medical Center
prn. Restraints were taken off and one to one sitter was present
with patient entire stay in hospital. Patient was discharged to
psychiatric facility for further care.

4. Left eye corneal abrasion: 04-08 contact lens being left in all
night.
Erythromycin Kirby Medical Center to left eye for 7 days.

5. FEN: house diet, IVF given over night.

6. Ppx: hep sc, diet

7. FULL CODE

Medications on Admission:
none, but acknowledged taking GHB, cocaine and amphetamines

Discharge Medications:
1. Heparin Sodium (Porcine) 5,000 unit/mL Solution Sig: One (1)
Injection TID (3 times a day).
2. Lansoprazole 30 mg Capsule, Delayed Release(E.C.) Sig: One
(1) Capsule, Delayed Release(E.C.) PO once a day.
3. Haloperidol 5 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO TID (3 times a
day).
4. Haloperidol 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed for aggitation.
5. Polyvinyl Alcohol 1.4 % Drops Sig: 1-2 Drops Ophthalmic  PRN
(as needed).
6. Erythromycin 5 mg/g Ointment Sig: One (1)  Ophthalmic  QID (4
times a day) for 6 days.
7. Haloperidol Lactate 5 mg/mL Solution Sig: 02-06  Injection Kirby Medical Center
(2 times a day) as needed for aggitation.
8. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q4-6H (every
4 to 6 hours) as needed.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
St Marys Janesville Hospital - 1312 Nelson Ramp
Huffmanborough, VI 32664

Discharge Diagnosis:
GHB overdose
multi-substance abuse
psychosis
paranoid delusions


Discharge Condition:
improved, alert and oriented, less agitated,
communicative-discharge to in-patient psych facilit
-no active medical issues

Discharge Instructions:
Please seek medical attention if you develop fevers, chills,
nausea, vomiting, chest pain, shortness of breath.
-please take medications as directed
-follow up with PCP
Mark Carter using [**Name9 (PRE) **]

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with your PCP and psychiatrist, please call to
schedule appointments
-PCP-Zachary Bennett
Tara Guerrero DDS
-substance abuse counseling
